skill.name: specdd-plan
skill.description: Use when Agent is asked to plan SpecDD work or needs to resolve unclear scope, authority, risk, or verification before editing.
skill.summary: Plan SpecDD work by resolving scope, specs, authority, risks, and verification before edits.
skill.author: specdd

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported shell backtick and weak cryptography patterns, but review found they are false positives in markdown text. No executable scripts, network behavior, credential access, prompt injection attempt, or malicious intent were found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backtick References
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick execution locations are inline markdown references to SpecDD files, skill names, or constraint labels. They are not executable code and provide no command injection path.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The marked lines are plain markdown instructions and file references. No code block, interpreter directive, shell execution, or user input path is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: The high severity weak cryptography alert points to the front matter description. The line contains natural language about planning and risk, not a cryptographic algorithm or implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is YAML metadata text and contains no hashing, encryption, random generation, or cryptographic library usage. This is a clear static pattern mismatch.

content.value_statement: SpecDD projects can fail when agents edit before resolving scope, authority, and governing specs. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to produce a practical plan before implementation.
actual_capabilities: Identifies when planning is required before editing a SpecDD project.
Directs the agent to resolve the active bootstrap chain.
Guides discovery of the effective spec chain for a target.
Requires write authority to be identified before file changes.
Structures plans around scope, likely files, verification, risks, and blockers.
limitations: Only applies to projects that already contain a root .specdd/bootstrap.md file.
Does not perform implementation work while the planning workflow is active.
Depends on the agent reading project specs accurately before decisions.
Does not replace project-specific contracts or human approval.

use_cases.title: Plan a SpecDD Feature
use_cases.description: Prepare a scoped implementation plan before changing files in a SpecDD-governed project.

use_cases.title: Resolve Write Authority
use_cases.description: Find the governing specs that permit or block a requested edit before work starts.

use_cases.title: Audit Risk Before Changes
use_cases.description: List unclear contracts, verification needs, and blockers before delegating implementation.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning for a clear SpecDD task.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use specdd-plan to plan this change: [describe the target behavior]. Include scope, governing specs, likely files, and verification.

prompt_templates.title: Check Authority First
prompt_templates.scenario: A task may need confirmation before edits.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use specdd-plan to determine whether this requested edit has write authority: [describe files or behavior]. Do not propose implementation until authority is clear.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Across Multiple Areas
prompt_templates.scenario: A change may touch several directories or specs.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use specdd-plan for this multi-area change: [describe change]. Resolve the effective spec chain for each target and identify conflicts.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a Verification Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced planning for risky or contract-heavy work.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use specdd-plan to produce an implementation readiness plan for [task]. Include blockers, risk decisions, required spec quotes, and verification steps.

output_examples.input: Plan a change to add validation to a SpecDD project module.
output_examples.output: A concise plan that names the target scope, governing bootstrap and specs, likely files, write authority status, verification steps, and open blockers.

output_examples.input: Check whether an agent can edit files under a new feature directory.
output_examples.output: An authority-focused planning summary that identifies the nearest governing spec, unclear permissions, and questions that must be answered before edits.

output_examples.input: Prepare implementation steps for a risky behavior change.
output_examples.output: A smallest safe path that separates required contract checks, likely file changes, verification, and risks that could block implementation.

best_practices: Use this skill before edits when scope, authority, or verification is unclear.
Provide the target path, behavior, or changed files so the spec chain can be resolved.
Keep the final plan narrow and tied to explicit SpecDD contracts.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill to adopt SpecDD in a project that lacks a root bootstrap file.
Do not skip reading governing specs when exact authority is required.
Do not turn the plan into broad refactors unless the specs or user request require them.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It helps an agent plan SpecDD work before editing by resolving scope, specs, authority, risks, and verification.

faq.question: Does it make code changes?
faq.answer: No. The skill explicitly keeps the agent in planning mode until implementation is separately authorized.

faq.question: When should I use it?
faq.answer: Use it when a SpecDD task has unclear scope, unclear write authority, high risk, or important verification needs.

faq.question: Does it work outside SpecDD projects?
faq.answer: No. It is intended only for projects with a root .specdd/bootstrap.md file.

faq.question: Can it use other SpecDD skills?
faq.answer: It may suggest consulting a CLI-focused SpecDD skill when available, but authority still depends on governing specs.

faq.question: Is it safe for marketplace use?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed file contains planning instructions only and no executable code, network calls, or secret access.
